title: Finding a corresponding angle
problem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle measures $85°$. Find the corresponding angle.

steps.instruction: Recall the definition of corresponding angles.
steps.working: Corresponding angles lie in matching positions at each parallel line — same side of the transversal, same location relative to the intersection.

steps.instruction: State the relationship between corresponding angles.
steps.working: When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding angles are equal.

title: Finding a co-interior angle
problem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle is $74°$. Find the other co-interior angle.

steps.instruction: Recall where co-interior angles are located.
steps.working: Co-interior angles lie between the parallel lines on the same side of the transversal.

steps.instruction: State the relationship between co-interior angles.
steps.working: Co-interior angles between parallel lines are supplementary — they add to $180°$.

title: Solving for an unknown using co-interior angles
problem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The co-interior angles are $(3x + 10)°$ and $(2x + 20)°$. Find the value of $x$.

steps.instruction: Write the equation using the co-interior angle relationship.
